free_text: Comparison of the quasifree charge-exchange reaction
Typical beam polarization 77% at 290 MeV and
medium resolution spectrometer. Protons at large
angles blocked by shielding or are bent by small
dipole magnet placed between target and recoil
scintillator. Thin plastic scintillator placed just
before recoil scintillator to veto protons or neutral
atomic hydrogen. Scattered neutrons detected using
CH2 liquid scintillator; scintillator material BC513
(BICRON). Two front end wire chambers placed after
recoil scintillator to track protons, followed by
quadrupole magnet. Beam polarization monitored using
in-beam polarimeter consisting of a thin CH2 target
and l=plastic scintillators to detect p-p elastic
first excited state peaks and the 12C(p,n)12N state
for 7Li(p,n) reaction to 7Be ground and 0.43 MeV
excited states used to check normalization.
